title: New York Fed Releases Report on Policy Actions in Response to the COVID-19 Pandemic

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The New York Fed released a special issue of its Economic Policy Review documenting the Federal Reserve's COVID-19 pandemic response, covering 10 distinct liquidity facilities and emergency programs including the CPFF, MMLF, PDCF, Corporate Credit Facilities, Main Street Lending Program, and Market Functioning Purchases. The volume comprises research articles assessing facility design, operational details, and market impact assessments developed from a September 2021 conference.
key_points: The volume covers 10 Fed facilities: CPFF, Central Bank Swap Lines, FIMA Repo Facility, Market Functioning Purchases, MSLP, MMLF, MLF, PPPLF, PDCF, and Corporate Credit Facilities (PMCCF and SMCCF)

Nine articles originated from the September 2021 conference 'Implications of Federal Reserve Actions in Response to the COVID-19 Pandemic' with participants from NY Fed, Federal Reserve Board, ECB, BIS, Treasury, and academia
Research assesses how Corporate Credit Facilities impacted corporate bond markets through facility design and usage analysis
Municipal Liquidity Facility (MLF) analysis shows the facility contributed to healing in municipal securities markets
Central Bank Swap Lines and FIMA Repo Facility provided evidence of reducing strains in global dollar funding and U.S. Treasury markets during extreme stress
MMLF is documented as an important tool in stemming investor outflows from money market funds and restoring calm in short-term funding markets
why_it_matters: This research volume provides comprehensive documentation of how the Federal Reserve deployed emergency liquidity facilities during the COVID-19 crisis, offering insights into market structure and plumbing during stress periods. Understanding these mechanisms is valuable for assessing potential Fed responses to future market disruptions.
market_structure_context: The volume documents Fed emergency facilities that functioned as critical market infrastructure during COVID-19 stress, including: Commercial Paper Funding Facility (CPFF) for short-term funding markets; Primary Dealer Credit Facility (PDCF) as emergency dealer financing; Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility (MMLF) forMMF stability; and Market Functioning Purchases for Treasury market liquidity. Research assesses how these facilities restored functioning across corporate bond, municipal, and money markets.

issues_found: Source is a press release announcing a research volume rather than the research papers themselves; however, the press release contains substantive summary information about facility designs, operations, and market impact assessments
Source age (June 2022) exceeds 90 days but has clear archival value as documentation of COVID-era Fed emergency facilities

human_review.notes: Historical source (2022) documenting COVID-era Fed facilities. This is a press release summarizing research rather than the research itself. Lower confidence due to age and indirect source material.

notes: Historical record: Published June 30, 2022 (approximately 1,369 days before TODAY). This is a press release announcing the release of a research volume on Fed COVID-19 emergency facilities, not the research papers themselves. The press release provides an overview of the topics covered but lacks the detailed quantitative findings from the underlying research articles. For deeper market structure insights, the individual Staff Reports referenced (available via the NY Fed's working paper series) would be more appropriate sources. Topic relevance confirmed: facilities documented (PDCF, CPFF, MMLF, swap lines) directly relate to market structure/market plumbing.
